reasoning: President Yamandú Orsi of Uruguay was formally invited by Brazilian President Lula da Silva to attend the BRICS summit in Rio de Janeiro in July 2025. Following the invitation, Uruguay's Foreign Minister Mario Lubetkin publicly praised the opportunity, stating that participating "will mean a change in the quality of the dialogue between our country and the great emerging countries of the South." Given Orsi's center-left political alignment with Lula, the geographic proximity of Rio de Janeiro, Uruguay's pending finalization of its accession to the BRICS New Development Bank, and strong economic incentives to engage with BRICS nations (such as China), it is highly likely that Orsi will accept. While there is a small chance diplomatic pressure from the U.S. might cause him to send a lower-level delegation (like the Foreign Minister) instead of attending in person, the public and positive governmental reception of the invitation strongly points to a personal attendance. Therefore, an announcement confirming his attendance before July 1, 2025, is highly probable.
